free_text: Cross sections of proton induced reactions on 61Ni.
5U Pelletron accelerator
The target was prepared by
vacuum evaporation of elemental Ni, enriched to
88.84% in 61Ni, from a previously outgassed tungsten
boat, on to an outgassed tantalum backing. The
backing was weighed before and after the evaporation
to give a measurement of target thickness. Target
thickness was determined also from alpha-particle
back-scattering measurements at an angle of 145 degr.
The adopted thickness value was
SAMPLE in sub 001 was corrected. FACILITY
was corrected. Data in sub 004 were redigitized. Data
format in sub 002-004 was changed.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
Beam currents were typically 500 nA and charge per
point 300 mu-C, corresponding to a bombardment period
of approximately 10 min.
The 125 cm3 Ge(Li) detector was located 3 cm
from the target in the 0 degr. direction.
Statistical uncertainty
Uncertainty in the cross section scale,
the main contributors being:
Uncertainty in the detection efficiency,
Uncertainty in the target thickness 5%,
Uncertainty in the beam current integration 4%.
Fig.2
Prompt gamma-ray measurement
SD: BIB updated. DATA-ERR -> ERR-1.
The neutron detector consisted of a block of
paraffin 32*32*44 cm3 containing six 3He-filled
proportional counters running parallel to the incident
beam. Two of the proportional counters, of active
length 15 cm, were mounted 8 cm from the target in the
horizontal plane and the remaining four, of active
length 30 cm, were mounted 15 cm from the target in
the 45 degr. diagonal planes.
For the neutron counting measurement, the target was
mounted in a stainless steel tubular cross which was
located centrally in the neutron detector.
Uncertainty in the cross section scale,
the main contributors being:
Uncertainty in the 61Cu branching ratios,
Uncertainty in the gamma-ray detection
efficiency,
Uncertainty in the neutron detection efficiency
Uncertainty in the target thickness,
Uncertainty in the beam current integration.
Fig.3
Activation measurement
SD: BIB updated. DATA-ERR -> ERR-1.
Gamma rays were detected by means of a 125cm3
Ge(Li) detector placed 3 cm from the target in the 55
degr. direction for proton energies below the neutron
threshold, and a 60 cm3 Ge(Li) detector similarly
placed for energies above the neutron threshold.
Statistical uncertainty
Uncertainty in the cross section scale is 10%,
the main contributors being
Uncertainty in the detection efficiency 7%,
Uncertainty in the target thickness 5%,
Uncertainty in the beam current integration 4%.
Fig.4
SD: BIB updated.
Thermonuclear reaction rates were calculated from the
experimental cross section data by means of the
expression of Fowler et al. (Ann. Rev. Astr. and
Astrophys. 5, 525, 1967). When cross sections were
required for energies outside the experimental range,
these were calculated by means of HAUSER*4 using the
Sevior parameters and reduced by factors of 1.19 for
the (p,gamma) reaction.
No information about source of uncertainty
Tbl.1
SD: SF9=CALC -> DERIV in REACTION code.
COMMENT -> ANALYSIS.
Thermonuclear reaction rates were calculated from the
experimental cross section data by means of the
expression of Fowler et al. (Ann. Rev. Astr. and
Astrophys. 5, 525, 1967). When cross sections were
required for energies outside the experimental range,
these were calculated by means of HAUSER*4 using the
Sevior parameters and reduced by factors of 1.14 for
the (p,n) reaction.
No information about source of uncertainty

Beam currents were typically 200 nA and charge per
point 200 mu-C.
Gamma rays were detected by means of a 125cm3
Ge(Li) detector placed 3 cm from the target in the 55
degr. direction for proton energies below the neutron
threshold, and a 60 cm3 Ge(Li) detector similarly
placed for energies above the neutron threshold.
Uncertainty in the cross section scale,
the main contributors being:
Uncertainty in the detection efficiency,
Uncertainty in the target thickness,
Uncertainty in the beam current integration.
Fig.2
Activation measurement
SD: BIB updated.
The bombardment periods of approximately 20 min were
followed by counting periods of 20 min.
The activation measurement was carried out
simultaneously with the (p,gamma) gamma-ray yield
measurement. Gamma rays were detected by means of
a 125cm3 Ge(Li) detector placed 3 cm from the target
in the 55 degr. direction for proton energies below
the neutron threshold, and a 60 cm3 Ge(Li) detector
similarly placed for energies above the neutron
threshold.
Uncertainty in the cross section scale,
the main contributors being:
Uncertainty in the 61Cu branching ratios,
Uncertainty in the gamma-ray detection
efficiency,
Uncertainty in the neutron detection efficiency
Uncertainty in the target thickness,
Uncertainty in the beam current integration.

Outgoing neutron measurement
